name: Use Pronounceable Names
source: Clean Code by Robert C. Martin, Chapter 2: Meaningful Names
problem: Identifier names are composed of abbreviations or concatenated syllables that cannot be naturally pronounced, making spoken discussion awkward and reducing readability for new team members.
steps: Replace unpronounceable abbreviations with full descriptive words.
Verify that every identifier name can be spoken naturally in a sentence.
Prefer compound words or short phrases over acronym-heavy abbreviations.
When reviewing code, read names aloud; if a name sounds unnatural, rename it.
Ensure new team members can discuss the code without resorting to spelling out identifiers.
